---
title: "Sounds, Phonemes, Words"
---

| Date | Topic | Reading | Resources
| -- | -- | -- | -- | 
| Tues 9/22  | Sounds | SGN Ch. 3: *Sounds in the Brain* | |
| Thurs 9/24 | Lateralization | Zatorre et al. 1992 |  |
| Tues 9/29 | Phonemes | Phillips et al. 2000 | |
| Thurs 10/1   | Words | SGN Ch. 5: *Activating Words* | |
| Tues 10/6 | Time course of word processing |  Marinkovic et al. (2003) | |
| Tues 10/6 | <span class="exam-badge">Exam #2</span> | |

::: {.callout-tip}
# Learning Goals: Unit 2

**Sounds & Phonemes**

1. Be able to define the following terms: *phoneme*, *phonetic feature*, *contrastive*, *categorical perception*, *speech envelope*, *entrainment*
1. Be able to identify the major brain regions that are involved in processing language sounds using neuroanatomical and directional terms
1. Be able to point to these regions on a picture of the brain
1. Describe the evidence for the following claim: Speech perception is categorical.
1. Name one area of current controversy

**Words**

1. Be able to define the following terms: *lexical item*, *morpheme*, *supramodal*
1. Identify the stages involved in processing spoken and written words
1. Be able to identify the major brain regions that are involved in processing words using neuroanatomical and directional terms
1. Describe evidence for the following claim: Spoken and written words activate a shared lexical-semantic processing system in the brain
1. Name one area of current controversy
:::
